cards.title: Balancing Work and Childcare This Summer
cards.body: With childcare costs rising, parents face the annual challenge of juggling work while school-aged children are home. The Guardian invites parents to share how they're managing the summer holiday period.

cards.title: Surrogacy and Fertility Journey Support
cards.body: Narzny McKinney shares her experience using a surrogate after eight rounds of IVF. The personal story addresses misconceptions surrounding surrogacy practices.

cards.title: Creating Meaningful Rituals for Children
cards.body: Jackie Bailey describes creating a special high tea ritual for her daughter's 12th birthday. The article explores the importance of marking milestones in children's lives.

cards.title: Teen Daughter's Self-Worth and Social Media
cards.body: Advice columnist Eleanor Gordon-Smith addresses how social media impacts sensitive teens' self-esteem. She emphasizes that unusual children will find their community as their world expands.

cards.title: When Should Your Neurodivergent Child Get a Phone?
cards.body: Focus on developmental readiness rather than age when deciding if your neurodivergent child is ready for a cell phone. Consider your child's individual maturity and needs.

cards.title: Discussing Mortality With Your Children
cards.body: Angelina Jolie has spoken with her children about her own mortality, raising important questions for parents. Experts discuss how and when to have these difficult conversations.

cards.title: Money Conversations After Divorce
cards.body: A family law attorney and divorced parent offer guidance on discussing finances with children after separation. Building financial stability helps ease household tension and teach money management.

cards.title: Building Empowerment After Difficult Childhood
cards.body: An empowered life is built through self-respect, boundaries, and small choices, especially after trauma. Practical tips guide parents and adults toward healing and empowerment.
